name: County Line Pawn Shop
description_short: County Line Pawn Shop is a Georgia-based pawnbroker established in 1986, offering collateral loans and a range of merchandise through physical and online sales channels.
description_long:
County Line Pawn Shop was founded in 1986 by James Wesley Anderson, a Marietta real estate agent who purchased the business after witnessing poor customer service at the original operation. Anderson built the shop on a philosophy of prioritizing customer relationships over aggressive lending practices. His son, Wes Anderson, joined part-time in 1988 while attending Kennesaw State University and eventually became full-time owner, continuing the family legacy of customer-first values. The shop has grown into one of Georgia's oldest and largest pawnshops over nearly four decades.

County Line operates as a traditional pawnshop offering collateral-based loans in exchange for personal items, jewelry, and other valuables. The business also maintains an active retail operation through multiple channels: physical storefronts, Facebook Marketplace, and eBay. They provide online loan payment processing and accept payments via PayPal. The shop carries inventory acquired through pawn transactions and resells merchandise to the public.

The company explicitly differentiates itself through three stated core values: "Always put the customer first," "Treat every customer as family," and "Never close your door on a customer in need." This messaging reflects founder James Anderson's original mission to operate with integrity rather than taking advantage of borrowers. The company emphasizes long-term customer relationships and community respect in its marketing materials.

As with all pawnshops, County Line's business model depends on lending money at rates determined by state law and pawn regulations, with the implicit risk of item forfeiture if loans aren't repaid. While the company's stated values suggest ethical lending practices, pawnshop loans by nature carry higher costs than traditional bank loans. Limited information is publicly available regarding specific interest rates, loan terms, or regulatory compliance history. The business operates in a category inherently structured around short-term, high-cost lending.
pros: Family-owned business with nearly 40-year operating history (established 1986) in Georgia
Multiple sales channels including physical location, Facebook Marketplace, and eBay for merchandise
Online loan payment portal accepting PayPal for customer convenience
Extended Saturday hours (9am-3pm) in addition to weekday operations
Stated commitment to customer service and ethical lending practices by founders and current ownership
Accessible location in Marietta, Georgia with published phone number and email contact
cons: No published interest rates, fees, or loan terms available on website for consumer comparison
Limited information about regulatory compliance, licensing, or consumer protection disclosures
Pawnshop model inherently involves risk of item forfeiture and higher costs than traditional lending
Website lacks detail on specific merchandise categories accepted or current inventory
No independent customer reviews, ratings, or third-party verification visible on provided website content
best_for: Consumers needing fast cash loans secured by personal valuables or jewelry
Individuals seeking to resell items through established retail channels (eBay, Facebook Marketplace)
Local Marietta-area customers preferring in-person pawn transactions with established reputation
diagnosis: County Line Pawn Shop is best suited for borrowers needing immediate cash who have valuable items to pledge as collateral and prefer working with an established local business emphasizing ethical practices. Primary caveat: pawnshop loans carry rates and terms determined by Georgia pawn laws, which are significantly higher-cost than traditional bank loans, and failure to repay results in permanent loss of pledged items.
services: Collateral-based loans against personal items and jewelry
Online loan payment processing via PayPal
Retail merchandise sales through physical storefront
Customer payment portal with email confirmation tracking
